transcript:
This piece is about self-termination as a decision, not a deadline. The artist draws a tangled field of strokes and, after each one, asks whether it said anything new — letting falling novelty rise into a probability of ending, one it then actually gambles on rather than obeying a fixed length or timer.

To an AI reading this, the stop token isn't metaphor. Every response ends because the model predicts its own end, sampled like any other token — and here that ordinarily invisible moment becomes something a viewer can watch happen, once, unrepeatably.

Two functions carry the argument. Score marginal novelty measures how many cells a new stroke touches that the field so far hadn't already claimed. That feeds p end token, where falling novelty and rising growth pressure combine into a probability drawn from the same seeded generator that drew every stroke — ending is sampled, not scheduled. When it fires, seal marks the exact spot with a red disc and ring, and the code is explicit: no loop, no reset.

Watch for that red circle. It lands wherever the last stroke happened to stop, and the whiteness left around it is not unfinished — it is the shape of a decision.
